message:
fix(ext4): inode-bitmap gate on read_inode + xattr ibody offset semantics

Two correctness fixes in the ext4 read path: gate read_inode on the
inode-allocation bitmap so deleted orphans correctly surface as
NotFound, and align the ibody xattr e_value_offs semantics between
the parser and writer.

crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:

- read_inode now reads gd.inode_bitmap first, optionally verifies
  its metadata checksum when the superblock has_metadata_csum(),
  and refuses to decode the inode table entry when the
  corresponding bit in the bitmap is clear. Returns
  FfsError::NotFound(format!("inode {}", ino.0)) instead of
  silently returning a zeroed / orphan-cleared inode.
- Crash-recovery test ext4_orphan_cleanup_marks_inodes_free
  updated to expect the new NotFound behaviour (previously it
  was checking that links_count/size were zeroed on a read).

crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs:

- Ext4ImageReader::read_xattrs_ibody and parse_ibody_xattrs now
  pass &inode.xattr_ibody (the full region including the 4-byte
  magic header) as the base for parse_xattr_entries' value
  slice, instead of entries (which skipped the 4-byte header).
  The ext4 on-disk contract says e_value_offs is measured from
  the start of the ibody xattr header, not from the start of
  the entries array; with the previous base, values written by
  the kernel or e2fsprogs would read from an offset that was 4
  bytes short of the right location.
- Doc comment + a test ibody assembly block corrected to reflect
  the "offset from start of ibody xattr header" rule.

crates/ffs-xattr/src/lib.rs:

- build_inline_ibody now passes INLINE_HEADER_LEN as the base
  offset to encode_entries_region so value_offs values come out
  relative to the ibody header, matching the fixed parser.
- Test build_inline_ibody_value_offset_is_relative_to_post_magic_
  region renamed to build_inline_ibody_value_offset_is_relative_
  to_ibody_header and its expected offset adjusted from 84 to 88,
  plus the indexing of the assertion slice dropped the old
  +4 / -4 adjustment.
